title: xetola transsylvanica
intro: Xetola transsylvanica, also known as Hungarian chamomile, is a perennial plant native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and soothing properties. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which have been used to treat various health issues such as anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Xetola transsylvanica include reducing stress and promoting relaxation, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal preparations of Xetola transsylvanica include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
